generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance BuildingTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT sensor measuring indoor/outdoor temperature for real-time building performance monitoring and energy optimization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData BuildingTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance BuildingHumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT sensor measuring indoor/outdoor humidity levels to support HVAC system optimization and occupant comfort analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData BuildingHumidityDataTransmitted
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Motion/PIR sensor detecting occupancy status in building spaces to enable demand-based lighting and HVAC control."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance HVACPerformanceSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Specialized sensor monitoring HVAC system metrics (e.g., airflow, pressure differentials) for predictive maintenance."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HVACPerformanceDataTransmitted
]

instance StructuralVibrationSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Structural health monitoring sensor detecting vibration patterns in building components for integrity assessment."
    DTDFVocab:producedData StructuralVibrationDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyConsumptionMeter : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Smart meter measuring real-time energy consumption across building systems for optimization and cost analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
]

instance EnvironmentalConditionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Multi-parameter sensor measuring external environmental conditions (e.g., wind, precipitation) affecting building performance."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalConditionDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance BuildingTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom BuildingTemperatureSensor
]

instance BuildingHumidityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom BuildingHumiditySensor
]

instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance HVACPerformanceDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HVACPerformanceSensor
]

instance StructuralVibrationDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom StructuralVibrationSensor
]

instance EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionMeter
]

instance EnvironmentalConditionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalConditionSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BIMGeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Static 3D/4D Building Information Model containing architectural, structural, and MEP system representations."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BIMIntegrationEnabler
]

instance BuildingControlSystemModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Semantically enriched BCS model integrating sensor/actuator configurations with BIM for cyber-physical synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelTransformationEnabler
]

instance EnergySimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Data-driven simulation model for predicting energy consumption patterns and optimization scenarios."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngineEnabler
]

instance StructuralHealthModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Analytical model for structural integrity assessment using vibration data and finite element analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo StructuralAnalysisEnabler
]

instance RealTimeOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated runtime data from all sensors and operational logs synchronized with the physical building state."
    DTDFVocab:fromData BuildingTemperatureDataTransmitted, BuildingHumidityDataTransmitted, OccupancyDataTransmitted, HVACPerformanceDataTransmitted, StructuralVibrationDataTransmitted, EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted, EnvironmentalConditionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataIntegrationEnabler, VisualizationEnabler
]

instance HistoricalPerformanceData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series historical data for trend analysis, predictive maintenance, and lifecycle performance assessment."
    DTDFVocab:fromData BuildingTemperatureDataTransmitted, EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PredictiveAnalyticsEnabler
]

instance BIMEnrichedMetadata : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Semantic metadata linking BIM elements to operational data streams for unified digital representation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData RealTimeOperationalData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BIMIntegrationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance BIMIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Driven Engineering framework for automated extraction and transformation of BIM models into deployable DT artifacts."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, VisualizationService
]

instance ModelTransformationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Automated pipeline converting BCS device models into runtime-ready digital twin components with minimal manual intervention."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveMaintenanceService, ControlAutomationService
]

instance DataIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Centralized data hub ensuring continuous bidirectional synchronization between physical assets and digital twin."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, AnomalyDetectionService
]

instance SimulationEngineEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "High-performance simulation platform for energy optimization, scenario testing, and what-if analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimizationService, EnergyForecastingService
]

instance StructuralAnalysisEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Specialized analytics engine for structural health monitoring using vibration data and finite element methods."
    DTDFVocab:enables StructuralIntegrityAssessmentService
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Machine learning and statistical modeling framework for forecasting asset degradation and operational trends."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveMaintenanceService, EnergyForecastingService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "3D/4D visualization platform integrating BIM models with real-time operational data for immersive monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService, CollaborationService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous aggregation and processing of runtime data to provide up-to-date building performance insights."
    DTDFVocab:provides BuildingPerformanceInsight, AnomalyDetectionInsight
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Data-driven forecasting of component degradation to enable proactive maintenance scheduling."
    DTDFVocab:provides ComponentDegradationInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides MaintenanceRecommendationAction
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Simulation-based optimization of building systems for improved operational efficiency and energy use."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyOptimizationInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemAdjustmentAction
]

instance ControlAutomationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Closed-loop integration with building automation systems for dynamic control of HVAC, lighting, and other systems."
    DTDFVocab:provides AutomationControlAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D/4D dashboards linking BIM models with real-time operational data for spatial-temporal analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides SpatialPerformanceInsight
]

instance CollaborationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Shared digital environment enabling multi-disciplinary collaboration across building lifecycle stages."
    DTDFVocab:provides StakeholderAlignmentInsight
]

instance AnomalyDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Pattern recognition system identifying deviations from expected building system behavior."
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyDetectionInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides AlertGenerationAction
]

instance StructuralIntegrityAssessmentService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Structural health monitoring service using vibration analysis to assess building component integrity."
    DTDFVocab:provides StructuralHealthInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides MaintenanceRecommendationAction
]

instance EnergyForecastingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics for forecasting energy consumption trends and optimization opportunities."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyConsumptionInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyOptimizationAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance BuildingPerformanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time operational state of building systems including temperature, humidity, occupancy, and energy metrics."
]

instance ComponentDegradationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted remaining useful life and degradation trends for critical building components based on sensor data."
]

instance EnergyOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identified opportunities for energy consumption reduction through system adjustments and operational changes."
]

instance SpatialPerformanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Spatial-temporal visualization of building performance metrics overlaid on BIM models for intuitive analysis."
]

instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identified deviations from expected system behavior with severity classification and potential root causes."
]

instance StructuralHealthInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Assessment of structural component integrity based on vibration analysis and finite element modeling."
]

instance EnergyConsumptionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Historical and predicted energy consumption patterns with cost analysis and optimization recommendations."
]

instance StakeholderAlignmentInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Shared understanding of building performance across disciplines through unified digital representation."
]

instance MaintenanceRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Proactive maintenance schedule with prioritized tasks based on predictive analytics."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance SystemAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated or recommended adjustments to building systems (e.g., HVAC setpoints, lighting schedules)."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AutomationControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Closed-loop control signals sent to building automation systems for dynamic system management."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AlertGenerationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated alerts for facility managers regarding detected anomalies or maintenance requirements."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EnergyOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Implemented energy-saving measures based on optimization analysis and predictive forecasting."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C1: Environment
instance AECOBuildingLifecycleEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Multi-phase building lifecycle environment spanning design, construction, operation, and refurbishment."
    base:contains BuildingDesignPhaseComponent, BuildingConstructionPhaseComponent, BuildingOperationPhaseComponent, BuildingRefurbishmentPhaseComponent
]

instance BuildingDesignPhaseComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Design phase component utilizing DT for simulation-based validation and optimization of building systems."
    base:isContainedIn AECOBuildingLifecycleEnvironment
]

instance BuildingConstructionPhaseComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Construction phase component leveraging DT for clash detection, sequencing optimization, and quality control."
    base:isContainedIn AECOBuildingLifecycleEnvironment
]

instance BuildingOperationPhaseComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Operational phase component using DT for real-time monitoring, predictive maintenance, and energy management."
    base:isContainedIn AECOBuildingLifecycleEnvironment
]

instance BuildingRefurbishmentPhaseComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Refurbishment phase component applying DT for adaptive design, lifecycle cost analysis, and system upgrades."
    base:isContainedIn AECOBuildingLifecycleEnvironment
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance HVACController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building automation system controller executing dynamic adjustments to HVAC systems based on occupancy and environmental conditions."
]

instance LightingController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Automated lighting control system adjusting illumination levels based on occupancy and natural light availability."
]

instance MaintenanceAlertSystem : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Automated system generating maintenance alerts and work orders based on predictive analytics and anomaly detection."
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this DT system is governed by a **control-centric architecture** where the digital twin issues commands to the physical building infrastructure. Key aspects include:
  - **Command Types**: Likely include **real-time control signals** (e.g., HVAC adjustments, lighting regulation, security system activations) derived from operational analytics or user inputs (e.g., Facility Managers).
  - **Control Message Formats**: Standardized formats are implied by the framework’s **interoperability requirements (NF_X)**, but specific formats (e.g., JSON, XML, binary) are not specified.
  - **Validation and Authentication Mechanisms**: Not explicitly detailed, though the framework’s **scalability and maintainability** requirements (NF_X) suggest robust mechanisms to prevent unauthorized or erroneous commands.
  - **Feedback Systems**: Bidirectional data exchange implies **closed-loop control**, where physical system responses (e.g., sensor confirmations, status updates) are fed back to the DT for validation or further adjustments.
  - **Conflict Resolution**: Likely handled via the **runtime configuration management** system, enabling dynamic adjustments without redeployment. Specific algorithms (e.g., priority-based arbitration) are not described.
  - **Command Execution Confirmation**: Implicit in the **real-time monitoring and control** workflow, where Facility Managers interact with the deployed DT to verify outcomes, though no explicit acknowledgment protocols are mentioned.
  "
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces **real-time data exchange capabilities** to ensure continuous synchronization between physical buildings and their digital counterparts, implying sub-second to millisecond-level latency requirements for critical operational data (e.g., sensor readings, control signals). The framework supports **dynamic temporal granularity** tailored to service-specific needs, such as high-frequency updates for monitoring (e.g., environmental sensors) and lower-frequency adjustments for configuration management. Runtime configuration management enables **on-the-fly adjustments** without redeployment, suggesting adaptive time scales for non-critical services (e.g., analytics or user interfaces). The system prioritizes **bidirectional, low-latency synchronization** to bridge static BIM representations with dynamic operational demands in the AECO domain, aligning with the need for seamless integration across the building lifecycle (e.g., from design to real-time control)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system employs a **coordinated, service-oriented constellation** centered on the DT Framework, which orchestrates interactions between **enriched BIM models**, **physical building components**, and **external services**. The architecture follows a **modular, role-based pattern** with explicit component integration:
  - **Bidirectional Data Flow**: The framework facilitates real-time synchronization between physical and digital layers, ensuring **holistic lifecycle management** (e.g., from design to operational phases).
  - **Runtime Configuration Management**: Enables dynamic adjustments to twin capabilities (e.g., adding/removing services) without full redeployment, leveraging **low-overhead updates** for maintainability.
  - **Resource Management**: The system prioritizes **scalability** from individual components (e.g., sensor data streams) to entire infrastructure systems, with **interoperability** ensured via standardized extension points.
  - **System-Wide Coordination**: The DT Framework acts as a **central hub**, resolving conflicts and managing dependencies between **Building Control Engineers**, **Facility Managers**, and **DT Engineers** through predefined roles and data exchange protocols. The constellation emphasizes **adaptability** to evolving operational requirements while maintaining **low-latency, high-fidelity synchronization** across the twin lifecycle."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the proposed model-driven deployment framework spans from **initial device modeling** to **custom DT service development**, addressing the gap between static Building Information Modeling (BIM) representations and dynamic DT requirements. Key phases include:
  1. **Device Modeling**: Building Control Engineers define sensors, actuators, and controllers within the BIM environment, creating a **semantic foundation** for subsequent transformations. This phase leverages BIM’s static capabilities to model Building Control System (BCS) components.
  2. **Model Transformation**: The framework employs **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles** to extract structured representations from enriched BIM models, enabling automated generation of deployable DT artifacts with **minimal manual intervention**.
  3. **DT Deployment**: The generated artifacts are deployed, establishing a **real-time synchronized** connection between the physical building and its digital twin. This phase ensures continuous bidirectional data exchange.
  4. **Operation & Monitoring**: Facility Managers interact with the DT to monitor system states and operate physical infrastructure, utilizing **interactive visualization** for immersive monitoring and control.
  5. **Custom Service Integration**: DT Engineers extend functionality through **custom services and plugins**, integrated via the framework’s **modular architecture** with standardized extension points. This phase supports evolutionary DT capabilities aligned with changing operational needs.
  Transition management between phases is governed by the framework’s **modular architecture**, ensuring seamless interoperability and lifecycle governance through standardized interfaces and automated workflows. The framework’s design is validated through research projects like **TwinLight and BOREALIS**, addressing practical deployment challenges."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin framework addresses **fidelity and validity** through a **multi-layered validation and verification (V&V) process** tied to its **model-driven deployment lifecycle**. **Model fidelity** is ensured by enriching static BIM representations with **dynamic BCS components** (sensors, actuators, controllers), enabling accurate behavioral and analytical modeling. **Validation methods** include automated **semantic consistency checks** during MDE-based model transformations and **runtime synchronization validation** to confirm real-time data alignment between physical and digital systems. **Uncertainty sources** are mitigated via structured data extraction from BIM, though explicit quantification methods (e.g., probabilistic models or error bounds) are not detailed. **Error detection** is implicit in the framework’s **real-time data exchange** and **interactive visualization**, where discrepancies trigger alerts for manual review or corrective actions. **Quality assurance** is embedded in the **modular architecture**, with standardized extension points enforcing compliance through custom service integration and analytics. The framework’s reliance on **BIM’s static limitations** suggests potential fidelity gaps, necessitating supplementary validation for dynamic operational scenarios (e.g., TwinLight/BOREALIS projects)."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical network infrastructure** supporting this DT system is designed for **heterogeneous interoperability** and **scalability**, with the following technical specifics:
  - **Communication Protocols**: Aligned with **non-functional requirements (NF_X)**, the system supports protocols enabling cross-platform integration (e.g., industry-standard protocols for IoT, BIM, or building automation), though exact protocols are not enumerated.
  - **Network Topology and Architecture**: The framework employs a **modular architecture** with **standardized extension points**, suggesting a **hybrid or service-oriented topology** where components (e.g., BIM tools, IoT sensors, analytics services) communicate via the central DT hub. The **bidirectional data exchange** implies a **star or mesh topology** for real-time synchronization.
  - **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**: Critical for **real-time monitoring and control**, but exact thresholds (e.g., <100ms latency, 10Mbps bandwidth) are not specified. The emphasis on **runtime adaptability** suggests dynamic resource allocation.
  - **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**: Implicit in the **scalability and maintainability** requirements (NF_X), though no specific redundancy strategies (e.g., failover nodes, data replication) are described.
  - **Security Protocols**: Not explicitly detailed, but the framework’s **accessibility for varying technical expertise** and **interoperability** imply adherence to industry security standards (e.g., encryption, access control) for network communication.
  "
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **deployment infrastructure** for this DT system is designed to support **dynamic operational needs** with the following technical attributes:
  - **Hosting Platforms**: Supports **cloud, edge, and on-premise** deployment models, though no specific platforms (e.g., AWS IoT, Kubernetes, local servers) are mentioned. The **modular architecture** and **runtime configuration management** enable flexible hosting choices.
  - **Computational and Storage Requirements**: Scalable to handle **individual components to entire infrastructure systems**, with low-overhead updates ensuring maintainability. Exact resource specifications (e.g., CPU cores, storage tiers) are not provided.
  - **Scalability Mechanisms**: Enabled by the **modular design** and **standardized extension points**, allowing incremental scaling of services and analytics without full redeployment. The framework’s **adaptability** suggests auto-scaling or elastic resource allocation.
  - **Deployment Models Supported**: Implicitly includes **hybrid models** (e.g., cloud-edge collaboration) and **on-premise** for sensitive or low-latency applications, though no specific deployment frameworks (e.g., Docker, Terraform) are referenced.
  - **Availability/Reliability Assurance**: Addressed through **non-functional requirements (NF_X)**, with mechanisms for continuous synchronization and runtime adjustments. No explicit SLAs (e.g., 99.9% uptime) or redundancy strategies (e.g., multi-region hosting) are detailed.
  "
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates horizontally with **external systems** through **interoperability standards** and **data exchange protocols**, ensuring seamless connectivity across heterogeneous environments. Key integration aspects include:
  - **BIM Tools**: Building Control Engineers use BIM platforms (e.g., Revit, ArchiCAD) to model devices and deploy Digital Twins, enabling **static-to-dynamic data translation** via enriched BIM models.
  - **Enterprise Systems**: The framework supports integration with **legacy and modern enterprise systems** (e.g., CMMS, ERP) through **standardized extension points**, allowing for **bidirectional data flows** (e.g., asset metadata, operational logs).
  - **Interoperability Standards**: The system adheres to **non-functional requirements (NF_X)** addressing cross-system compatibility, including **scalability** (from components to infrastructure) and **accessibility** for users with varying technical expertise.
  - **Dependencies**: External systems may provide **sensor data**, **control signals**, or **analytical services**, with the DT Framework managing dependencies via **runtime configuration** and **modular service integration**. Integration patterns include **event-driven updates** (e.g., real-time alerts) and **batch synchronization** (e.g., historical data for analytics). The system avoids vendor lock-in by prioritizing **open standards** and **low-overhead updates** for maintainability."
]
